page.title: FAQ
page.subtitle: Quick answers to what people actually ask

cta.title: Can't find an answer?
cta.subtitle: Join our Telegram group — devs reply directly, share bugs, see what others are asking.

items.a: 0.1% of the input SOL is sent to the Ocufi vault when you buy. Selling is currently free in V1 to avoid dust meme tokens piling up.

The max price deviation you accept between quote and execution.

Suggested: stables 0.5% / blue chips 1% / memes & pumps 3-5% (up to 10% for extremely thin liquidity).

Ocufi auto-suggests sane defaults by token type; you can override per trade.

Generic OKX fallback, not informative. Common causes:

1. Slippage too tight — raise to 5% or 10%
2. 100% sell balance race — Ocufi auto-leaves 0.1% dust
3. Not enough SOL for gas — keep ≥ 0.005 SOL
4. Pump.fun micro-liquidity

Phantom or Solflare (native Solana wallets) work best.

OKX Web3 is supported but has minor compatibility limits with advanced Jupiter features.

items.q: Can I use it on mobile?
items.a:
Yes, but Safari / Chrome can't see wallet extensions on mobile.

The fix: open www.ocufi.io inside the Phantom or Solflare app's built-in browser. The homepage detects mobile and shows an "Open in Phantom" button for one-tap deeplink.
Jupiter requires ≥ $5 USD per order. No workaround in V1.

items.q: Why am I not getting notifications?
items.a:
V1 supports browser notifications only — must keep Ocufi tab open.

Backend scans prices every 60s, frontend syncs every 20s (max ~1 min delay).

Email & Web Push are planned for Day 12+ so you can be offline.

+10 points for each successful buy / sell / limit order.

Capped at 100 points per wallet per day (anti-abuse). Each tx counted once.

items.q: What can I do with points?
items.a:
V1: leaderboard only (social bragging rights).

V2 plans (TBD): fee discounts, raffles, airdrop whitelist, tier membership. Follow @Ocufi_io for updates.

items.q: Can Ocufi access my private key?
items.a:
Never. Ocufi only builds transaction data; signing happens entirely in your wallet app. Private keys never leave your wallet.

Frontend code is open-source — audit yourself at github.com/rowanrowan548-maker/ocufi-web

items.q: What does the safety review check?
items.a:
Ocufi integrates RugCheck + internal allowlist to show pre-trade:

· Top 10 holder concentration (>80% = dangerous)
· LP lock status (unlocked = rug risk)
· Mint authority (not renounced = can dilute you)
· Freeze authority (not renounced = can freeze your tokens)
· Age (less than 1 hour old = high risk)

Result condensed into 5-tier rating: verified / low / medium / high / critical.
Free to use. 0.1% of each trade covers platform costs. No subscription, no VIP, no ads.

Twitter: @Ocufi_io
Telegram: t.me/+HucmvmOx2IswZDBl (live discussion / feedback / bug reports)
GitHub: github.com/rowanrowan548-maker/ocufi-web/issues

Ocufi never touches your funds. Every trade is signed locally in your wallet (Phantom / Solflare / OKX) and broadcast directly to Solana.

No account, no custody — just your on-chain address.

A Solana on-chain trading terminal.

· Non-custodial — your keys never leave your wallet
· Low fees — 0.2% (half what gmgn/bullx charge)
· Safety review — every token pre-checked via RugCheck + allowlist
· Open-source — frontend auditable on GitHub

docs.title: Docs
docs.subtitle: Get up and running with Ocufi in 5 minutes

connect.title: Connect a wallet
connect.body:
Phantom / Solflare / OKX supported. On desktop, click "Connect wallet" in the top right and pick your installed wallet extension.

On mobile you must open ocufi.io inside your wallet app's built-in browser — Safari/Chrome cannot detect wallet extensions.

buy.title: Buy a token
buy.body:
On the trade page, search by name / symbol / contract address. Pick a token to see the chart and safety check.

Buy form pays in SOL by default. Enter an amount (or click 0.1 / 0.5 / 1 / MAX), pick slippage and priority fee, click confirm. The dialog previews tokens out / price impact / min received before your wallet signs.

sell.title: Sell a token
sell.body:
Reach the sell form from portfolio or any token detail page. The form auto-loads your balance — click 25% / 50% / 100% for quick amounts.

Note: 100% uses raw on-chain BigInt to avoid floating-point dust mismatches that cause insufficient-balance failures.

limit.title: Limit orders
limit.body:
Switch the inner tab on the trade form to "Limit". Set a target price + amount and Jupiter auto-fills when reached.

Lifecycle: pending → waiting → filled or cancelled. The Limit tab shows real-time status.

safety.title: Safety check
safety.body:
Paste any contract address to see a full free RugCheck report: mint authority, freeze authority, LP lock %, top-10 concentration.

Red = multiple danger flags or already rugged. Green verified = in our maintained whitelist (USDC, JUP, TRUMP, etc. — legitimate centralized tokens).

watchlist.title: Watchlist
watchlist.body:
Star ⭐ a token from any list or detail page. Favorites live only in your browser localStorage — never uploaded.

The "Watchlist" link in the top nav opens the full list with live prices.

errors.title: Common errors & troubleshooting
errors.intro: 5 common issues — symptom → cause → fix. If your error isn't listed, search /faq or ask in the TG group.

txFailed.title: Transaction failed / on-chain revert
txFailed.symptom: After signing, you see 'failed' or red status on the explorer. Funds return to wallet (no loss, but gas spent).
txFailed.cause: Slippage exceeded / pool ran out of liquidity / Solana congestion above your priority fee.
txFailed.fix: Retry + raise slippage (5-10% for low-cap) + pick Fast or Turbo priority. After 3 fails, consider switching coin or waiting for the market to settle.

holdingDelay.title: Holdings page missing the coin you just bought
holdingDelay.symptom: Trade filled (visible on explorer) but /portfolio doesn't show the token.
holdingDelay.cause: Helius RPC index lag of 5-30s; new SPL tokens occasionally take 1-2 min.
holdingDelay.fix: Wait 30-60s and tap refresh. Still missing? Go directly to /trade?mint={addr} — selling still works.

withdraw.title: Invite rebate withdrawal feels slow
withdraw.symptom: You clicked Claim on /invite but SOL hasn't arrived in your wallet.
withdraw.cause: Invite withdrawal is server-side accounting + periodic batch on-chain transfer, not instant. Batches run 1-2× per week.
withdraw.fix: Watch the 'Pending review' field on /invite. Once approved, expect funds within 7 days. Stuck? Ping the TG group.

slippage.title: Slippage protection rejected the trade
slippage.symptom: After clicking Confirm Buy, you see 'Price moved X%, exceeded slippage'.
slippage.cause: Market moved too fast — actual fill drifted past your tolerance.
slippage.fix: Wait a few seconds and retry / raise slippage / split into smaller orders. For low-cap tokens, 5-10% slippage is reasonable.

walletDisconnect.title: Wallet keeps disconnecting / can't connect
walletDisconnect.symptom: After refresh wallet shows disconnected, or Connect button does nothing.
walletDisconnect.cause: Desktop Chrome incognito / iOS Safari / outdated wallet-app browser.
walletDisconnect.fix: Desktop: regular Chrome + Phantom/Solflare extension. Mobile: must use the wallet app's built-in browser (regular Safari can't detect wallets).

advanced.title: Advanced
advanced.intro: Power-user tweaks for tighter on-chain execution.

priorityFee.title: Priority fee tiers · Pilot / P1 / P2 / P3
priorityFee.body:
Pilot (lowest, slow) for non-urgent small orders. P1 (0.00005 SOL, fast) is V1 default — fits most cases. P2 (0.0005 SOL, very fast) for congested markets or hot launches. P3 (0.005 SOL, max) for rare drops or MEV-protect tier.

During Solana congestion, P1 may not land — bump to P2/P3 to land reliably.

mev.title: MEV protection (educational)
mev.body:
MEV (Maximal Extractable Value) = sandwich attackers front-running. They raise price before your buy, depress after your sell, pocket the spread.

Ocufi V1 routes through Jupiter; some liquidity flows through MEV-protected pools (Jito etc.). V2 plans dedicated MEV defense (Helius MEV Protect routes). Today's tip: high slippage helps attackers — use 1-3% slippage + high priority fee to land safely.

copyTrade.title: Copy trading (V2 preview)
copyTrade.body:
V2 plan: bind a target wallet, automatically mirror its trades (proportional sizing + risk filter + slippage cap).

Today: paste any wallet on /history to see its trade log; decide manually whether to follow.

docs.footer: More questions? Check the FAQ or open a GitHub Issue. All code is open and auditable.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.s1: Copy your invite link and share it. When someone first connects their wallet through your link, they're permanently bound to you.
howItWorks.s2: Once they make a trade ≥ $100, the invite is auto-activated.
howItWorks.s3: After activation, every trade they make earns you +10% of their points — forever, not a one-time bonus.
howItWorks.s4: Points redeem for merch; may unlock more perks later.
howItWorks.note: We don't share trading fees (0.2% is the moat). We share points — printed by us, near-zero cost, sustainable long-term.

s1.b:
Ocufi never touches your private keys or seed phrase. Every transaction is signed locally in your wallet extension (Phantom / Solflare / OKX) and sent directly to Solana.

Our servers only see: your wallet public key + your submitted transaction signatures (public on-chain data).

s2.b:
Before any buy, we hit two independent safety APIs in parallel:

· RugCheck: LP lock / holder concentration / mint+freeze authority / rug history
· GoPlus: transfer fee / honeypot / mutable balance / creator reputation

Either source flagging critical → hard block (honeypot / rugged / transfer fee ≥10% / liquidity <$10K / new pool <1h).

s3.b: Both frontend and backend are wired to Sentry error tracking. Any production error pages us automatically — we see it, fix it, and document the lesson in our public KNOWN_ISSUES.md.

s4.b: Price alerts / trade outcomes / system warnings can be pushed to your Telegram via @Ocufi_bot. Binding flow lives at /alerts · 3 steps · unbind anytime.

s5.b: Every trade includes a Solscan link (history / triggered alerts / trade results) — you can independently verify every number on-chain. Fee collection address and trade contract addresses are public, open to third-party audit.

s6.b: Ocufi is in the Phantom Portal review pipeline (the "Verified" badge in the directory). Once approved, users opening ocufi.io inside Phantom will see the official blue checkmark. See #devlog for current status.

s7.b:
Ocufi charges 0.1% on buys + 0% on sells (target 0.2% total) · paid directly to a public treasury address, fully verifiable on-chain.

Competitors (GMGN / Photon / BullX) typically charge 0.8-1%. We've cut V1 fees to 1/4-1/5 of the industry — long-term user trust > short-term revenue.

s1.b:
Ocufi is a non-custodial trading terminal. We never ask for an account, never store your private keys, never store your seed phrase.

The following pass through our service:
· Your wallet public key (provided by your browser wallet extension, used to sign tx)
· Your trade history (public on-chain data, visible to anyone on Solscan)
· Standard HTTP headers (IP, User-Agent) produced by Vercel / RPC nodes
· Favorite token mints (stored only in your browser localStorage; our servers never see them)

s2.b:
We do not collect: private keys, seed phrases, email, phone, KYC documents, biometrics, social accounts, device fingerprints.

If any page claims to be Ocufi but asks for these, it is a phishing site — close it and reach the real product through ocufi.io.

s3.b:
Jupiter Aggregator (quote + routing), DexScreener (market data), GeckoTerminal (trade feed), RugCheck (token safety), Helius / Solana RPC (on-chain access), Vercel (hosting).

Each has its own privacy policy. We do not actively forward anything beyond public on-chain data to them.

s4.b:
We use browser localStorage for a few preferences (favorites, slippage profile, locale). These never leave your browser.

We do not use third-party tracking cookies, do not show ads, do not integrate Google Analytics. The backend API keeps short-lived session logs (max 30 days) for debugging only.

s5.b:
When you complete a trade with your wallet, we record points server-side keyed by wallet address (wallet, tx signature, timestamp). This data is used only for the leaderboard (addresses are masked) and is not shared with third parties.

You can stop using Ocufi any time. There is no "delete account" button because we never created an account — only a few public on-chain records associated with your wallet.

s6.b: Questions about this policy? Open a GitHub Issue (github.com/rowanrowan548-maker/ocufi-web) or email support@ocufi.io (placeholder, V2).

s1.b: Ocufi provides an open-source frontend that helps you interact with on-chain DEX aggregators (Jupiter) on Solana. Your funds always remain in your wallet. All transactions are signed by you in your wallet and broadcast on-chain. Ocufi does not custody your funds, does not place orders on your behalf, and cannot reverse confirmed on-chain transactions.

s2.b:
You must verify yourself that:
· The wallet address, trade contents, and contract address are correct
· The token you are buying is not prohibited in your jurisdiction
· You understand on-chain transactions are irreversible once broadcast
· You comply with the tax, AML, and CFT laws of your jurisdiction

Ocufi is not responsible for your decisions.

s3.b: Ocufi charges a 0.1% SOL fee on each buy (collected via SystemProgram.transfer to the Ocufi vault before the swap). Sells are free in V1. All fees are transparent and the deployed code is fully auditable on GitHub.

s4.b: Do not use Ocufi for money laundering, fraud, market manipulation, or unauthorized financial activity. Do not scrape our endpoints, DoS our or any third-party APIs, or bypass any safety mechanism. We reserve the right to block IPs or wallet addresses from our frontend on detection.

s5.b:
Ocufi software is provided "AS IS" without any warranty. We are not liable for:
· Token devaluation, liquidity rug pulls, abandoned projects
· Solana network congestion, RPC outages, failed transactions
· Third-party API issues (Jupiter, DexScreener, RugCheck, Helius, etc.)
· Mistyped addresses or phishing attacks

Our maximum aggregate liability for any direct or indirect damage caused by use of this service is zero USD.

s6.b: We may modify, suspend, or sunset any feature without notice. Significant changes will be announced on-site or via Twitter/TG. Continued use implies acceptance of the latest terms.

s7.b: Interpretation, validity, and dispute resolution under these terms follow the law of the jurisdiction where the code is hosted. For on-chain disputes, the chain record is authoritative.

s1.b:
All prices, liquidity, market cap, risk scores, and holder data shown in Ocufi come from third-party public data sources (DexScreener, RugCheck, GeckoTerminal, Solana RPC). They are presented for information only and **do not constitute investment advice**.

Whether to buy, how much to buy, when to sell — all of this is your decision.

s2.h: Data may be stale or wrong
s2.b:
We try to keep data fresh, but third-party APIs may lag, rate-limit, or return wrong data. Always defer to Solscan / your wallet's actual on-chain values.

Before trading, double-check the contract address — there are too many tokens with the same name; only the contract address is the true identity.

s3.b: The "safety check" score on the token detail page is based on data from RugCheck (mint authority, LP lock %, top-10 concentration, etc.). This is a **rule-based approximation**: it can over-flag legitimate tokens (e.g. USDC keeps freeze authority for compliance) and can miss novel scams. **Passing the check does NOT mean it is safe to invest**.

s4.h: You may lose your entire principal
s4.b:
Crypto-asset prices are highly volatile, especially memecoins and newly launched tokens. **Only trade with funds you can afford to lose entirely**.

Common loss vectors: rug pulls, sharp dumps, slippage, buying scam contracts, private-key theft, malicious approvals, malicious wallet extensions.

s5.b: You must understand and comply with the cryptocurrency holding, trading, and reporting laws of your jurisdiction. Ocufi makes no compliance determinations or tax calculations on your behalf.
